content:
Hello, noob kernel dev here.

I have a patch I'm ready to send, but I'm unsure of who to send it to. I've been reading MAINTAINERS file as suggested in docs, but not finding the area for my change.

The area is rust/kernel/uaccess.rs how should I go about finding the right people to mail this patch to?

Sorry for dumb question

you can use the scripts/get_maintainer.pl your-patch.patch (you can generate your-patch.patch by using git format-patch, you'll also have to do that for sending the patch) to find the correct maintainer(s).
you can also run scripts/get_maintainer.pl rust/kernel/uaccess.rs to only check for that single file and not the entire change that you have
(in this case it will point you at the RUST maintainer entry)
This topic was moved here from #General > Find Who To Mail Patch To by Benno Lossin.

@Benno Lossin Thanks, I totally missed the scripts part, was manually going through the MAINTAINERS file haha.

Another question, I see 2 suporters and a bunch of reviewers (including you :) haha) . I'm assuming the reviewers are the CC addresses and the supporters are the TO addresses? or how does that work?

Thanks for the help by the way!

Antonio Hickey said:

Benno Lossin Thanks, I totally missed the scripts part, was manually going through the MAINTAINERS file haha.

Another question, I see 2 suporters and a bunch of reviewers (including you :) haha) . I'm assuming the reviewers are the CC addresses and the supporters are the TO addresses? or how does that work?

Thanks for the help by the way!

I'm not sure what you mean by "supporters", maybe you mean the maintainers in the M: entries? I don't actually know if there is a hard rule by which to decide if to use TO or CC... But I have seen mixes of both. The most important part is that you send the mail to the list and the people listed in the entry.

I use the following configuration in git:

[sendemail]
    tocmd = scripts/get_maintainer.pl --norolestats --nogit-fallback --nol
    cccmd = scripts/get_maintainer.pl --norolestats --nogit-fallback --nom --nor

Then it automatically picks the correct maintainers for the patch (but no guarantees of course :) when sending via git send-email. (it puts reviewers and maintainers in TO and the list in CC)

Have you also seen https://docs.kernel.org/process/submitting-patches.html ? It contains a lot of other useful information.
